content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

April 6, 1990

Lezlee H. Westine  
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON,  
PARRINELLO & MUELLER  
591 Redwood Highway #4000  
Mill Valley, CA 94941

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
No. A-90-175 191

Dear Ms. Westine:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of telephone advice I gave to you on February 23, 1990. Specifically, you want to confirm that it is permissible under the Political Reform Act (the "Act")1 for organizers of the Crime Victims' Initiative to send some of the Initiative's contributors to a Pete Wilson for Governor 1990 fundraiser at no charge to either the contributors or the Initiative committee.

Your primary concern in this activity is whether it constitutes a contribution to the Initiative committee from the Wilson campaign or vice versa. I indicated that as long as free admission to the Wilson fundraiser was offered to Initiative contributors after they made their contributions, the Initiative committee made no contribution to the Wilson campaign and the Wilson campaign made no contribution to the Initiative committee. In the latter instance, the benefit of any expenditure made by the Wilson committee in hosting the Initiative's contributors at the fundraiser flowed directly to the contributors and not the Initiative committee.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:SH:aa
